subject: Databases
title: INNER JOIN Basics
description: Learn INNER JOIN Basics in one minute.

hook.text: Users in one table, orders in another. INNER JOIN combines them where a user has matching orders.

buildup.text: SELECT * FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id. Rows with matching IDs are combined.

discovery.text: INNER JOIN only returns rows that have matches in both tables. No match? The row is excluded.

twist.text: A user with no orders vanishes from the result. That's by design with INNER JOIN.

climax.text: INNER JOIN is the most common join type. It's the default when you write just JOIN.

punchline.text: INNER JOIN: only matching rows from both sides.

quiz.question: What happens to a user with no orders in an INNER JOIN between users and orders?
options: The user is excluded from the results entirely
The user appears with NULL order values
